title: After-Hours Administrative Activity

compliance.assurance_rationale: Detects administrative activity outside normal business hours, which may indicate unauthorized account usage.
compliance.controlObjective: Flags administrative actions occurring outside approved maintenance windows or business hours.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search UC-5.13.49 scheduled daily; results exported to security review dashboard for SOC triage.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Supports access review by identifying administrative activity patterns outside normal business operations.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides evidence of after-hours access monitoring for IT general control reviews.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Weekly summary of after-hours admin actions by user, exported to the ITGC evidence pack.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:audit:logs`. from the `audit_logs` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/data/api/v1/event/event-series/audit-log`. Default poll interval: 300s (5m). Volume: Variable; ~10-100 events/day in typical environments. RBAC: SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `auditUserName` (string), `auditRequestType` (string), `auditDescription` (string), `auditParentId` (string), `createdDateTime` (epoch milliseconds), `isSystemEvent` (boolean). CIM data model: Change.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" | eval hour=strftime(_time, "%H") | eval day=strftime(_time, "%a") | where (hour<6 OR hour>22) OR (day="Sat" OR day="Sun") | stats count as after_hours_actions values(auditRequestType) as action_types by auditUserName, auditIpAddress | sort -after_hours_actions
description: Identifies administrative actions performed outside normal business hours (before 6am, after 10pm, or on weekends), which may indicate unauthorized access.
value: After-hours admin activity is a common indicator of insider threats, compromised accounts, or unauthorized maintenance. Flagging it enables rapid investigation.
implementation: Enable the `audit_logs` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA polls audit log data from the Catalyst Center Intent API `/dna/intent/api/v1/audit/logs` every 5 minutes. Key fields: `auditRequestType`, `auditDescription`, `auditUserName`, `auditTimestamp`, `auditIpAddress`.
visualization: Table (auditUserName, auditIpAddress, after_hours_actions, action_types), timeline panel for manual review, optional alert to SOC when a known admin baseline changes.

references.title: Catalyst Center Audit Log API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**CAB-approved maintenance window scheduled outside business hours.** Network teams often perform maintenance during nights and weekends to minimize user impact. These are authorized, planned activities. Distinguish by correlating with ITSM change records or approved maintenance schedules. Suppress by integrating a `catalyst_maintenance_windows` lookup and suppressing after-hours alerts for activities within approved change windows.

**Global team operating in a different time zone.** In multinational organizations, administrators in other time zones may perform legitimate work during what appears to be after-hours in the local time zone. Distinguish by checking whether the `username` corresponds to a known team member in a different time zone. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_admin_timezones` lookup and applying per-user time zone-aware business hours.

**Automated task scheduled during off-peak hours.** Catalyst Center's scheduled tasks (backup, compliance scan, template sync) may run during off-peak hours by design. Distinguish by checking whether the `username` is a system or service account performing scheduled operations. Suppress by filtering system accounts and known automation from the after-hours detection.

**On-call administrator responding to an alert outside business hours.** When an on-call engineer responds to an alert, their Catalyst Center activity will appear as after-hours activity. This is expected and authorized. Distinguish by correlating with on-call schedules or alert response tickets. Do not suppress — log and report the activity, but tag it as on-call response rather than suspicious activity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **audit_logs** input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:audit:logs`.
• Define business hours for your organization: specify the time zone, start hour, and end hour. For global organizations, maintain per-user time zones in a lookup.
• Identify known after-hours activities: maintenance windows, on-call response, global team operations in other time zones.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
The TA polls the audit log API at the configured interval. After-hours detection requires augmenting each audit event with a business-hours classification.

Key fields:
• `username`: the administrator performing the action.
• `createdAt` or `_time`: timestamp of the audit entry.
• `auditResourceType`: the type of resource accessed or modified.
• `isSystemEvent`: exclude system events from human activity analysis.
• `sourceIP`: may help identify the administrator's location/time zone.

Business hours definition example: Monday-Friday, 07:00-19:00 local time. Customize per your organization.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" isSystemEvent!=true
| eval hour=strftime(_time, "%H"), day_of_week=strftime(_time, "%w")
| eval is_after_hours=if((hour<7 OR hour>=19) OR (day_of_week=0 OR day_of_week=6), "yes", "no")
| where is_after_hours="yes"
| stats count as action_count values(auditResourceType) as resource_types values(auditDescription) as actions by username
| sort -action_count
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`eval hour=strftime(_time, "%H")`**: Extracts the hour (0-23) from the event timestamp. This uses the search head's time zone by default — ensure it matches your business hours definition.
• **`eval is_after_hours=if((hour<7 OR hour>=19) OR (day_of_week=0 OR day_of_week=6), ...)`**: Classifies events as after-hours if they fall outside 07:00-19:00 or on weekends (0=Sunday, 6=Saturday). Adjust the hours and days for your organization.
• **`where is_after_hours="yes"`**: Filters to only after-hours activity for investigation.
• **`values(auditResourceType) as resource_types`**: Shows what types of resources were accessed after hours — CREDENTIAL and USER changes after hours are higher risk than TEMPLATE reads.

For time zone-aware detection: `| lookup catalyst_admin_timezones username OUTPUT user_tz | eval local_hour=strftime(_time, "%H", user_tz)` — applies per-user time zones from a lookup.

Step 3 — Validate
• **Test event:** Make a configuration change in Catalyst Center outside your defined business hours. Verify the change appears in the after-hours results with the correct classification.
• **Baseline:** Run the search over 30 days and identify the typical after-hours activity pattern. Document legitimate after-hours users (global team members, on-call engineers).
• **Vendor UI parity:** cross-reference the Splunk results with the corresponding **Catalyst Center > Assurance** page for the same time window to confirm counts and values match.
Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** After-hours activity timeline with username color-coding, table of recent after-hours actions with drill-down to audit details.
• **Alert:** Trigger when a username not in the `catalyst_known_afterhours_users` lookup performs activity outside business hours. Exception: suppress during approved maintenance windows using a `catalyst_maintenance_windows` lookup.
• **Compliance:** Generate monthly reports of after-hours privileged activity for security review.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Global team members always appearing as after-hours:** Maintain a `catalyst_admin_timezones` lookup with each administrator's time zone and apply per-user business hours.
• **System events passing through the filter:** Verify `isSystemEvent!=true` is correctly filtering. Some Catalyst Center versions use `isSystemEvent="false"` (string) — check with `| fieldsummary isSystemEvent`.
• **Maintenance window activity flagged:** Integrate with the `catalyst_maintenance_windows` lookup to suppress during approved change windows.
• If data is not arriving for `cisco:dnac:audit:logs`, check that the `audit_logs` input is enabled in the TA configuration and that the Catalyst Center API credentials have not expired.
grandmaExplanation: We flag any Catalyst Center admin work done late at night or on weekends, because that is unusual and could mean someone is up to no good.
